access.anon: Can copy a single-section iCal link from the public section detail page and can retrieve a personal feed only when holding its token-bearing URL.
access.user: Can copy their own personal subscription iCal link.
access.agent: After authorization, can read the current user's personal calendar subscription information; REST and MCP should both return the same subscription feed information and description.

rules.copyable-links: iCal links should be presented as copyable links rather than requiring users to manually construct them.
rules.no-second-model: iCal is an export / subscription capability and should not carry a second business model.
rules.rfc5545-dtstamp-utc: Every VEVENT in section, multi-section, and personal feeds serializes DTSTAMP as a UTC DATE-TIME with a Z suffix while retaining Asia/Shanghai TZID values for event start and end times.
rules.personal-feed-cache: Personal feed responses may be served from a short-lived private server-side cache, include ETag, return 304 Not Modified for matching If-None-Match, and write cache hit/miss Cloudflare Analytics Engine datapoints without user IDs or feed tokens.
rules.static-json-resilient: Calendar location and building image enhancements only read static JSON published at https://static.life-ustc.tiankaima.dev; when loading fails or data is corrupted, diagnostic information should be logged and a valid iCal should still be returned.

notes: Returns text/calendar synthesized from the section's schedule and exams.
Same-user session or bearer access is accepted.
Anonymous feed access is accepted with either ?token=... or the userId:token path segment form.

tools.name: get_my_calendar_subscription
notes: Default mode returns feed links plus current-semester section context instead of the full subscribed section list.
The deprecated summary input is an alias for the same compact default response.
In default MCP output, token-bearing calendarPath and calendarUrl may be redacted for privacy; request full mode only when the raw feed location is explicitly needed.
Generates a multi-section iCal based on sectionIds query parameter.
